chapter_title.en: Chapter 92 – The perfume for the one in Ihraam

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/311)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/311)
text: لَا تَمَسَّ رَيْحَاناً وَ أَنْتَ مُحْرِمٌ وَ لَا شَيْئاً فِيهِ زَعْفَرَانٌ وَ لَا تَطْعَمْ طَعَاماً فِيهِ زَعْفَرَانٌ .

en.hubeali: Abu Ali Al Ashary, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Jabbar, from Safwan, from Abdullah Bin Sinan,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘Do not smell the aromas and you are in Ihraam, nor anything wherein is saffron, nor eat a meal with saffron in it’.
en.sarwar: 12. Safwan has narrated from abu al-Mighra’ who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who in the state of Ihram washes his hand with saltwort. He (the Imam) said, ‘My father, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, would wash his hands with white al-Hurud (saltwort).”’
